name: Indian IT Sector (TCS/Infosys/Wipro)
summary: Day 61 Tuesday. SAMSUNG Q1 PRELIM RECORD 57.2T WON (+755% YoY) REINFORCES GLOBAL AI-CAPEX BACKDROP; BRANCH 3 THESIS FALSIFIED IN OIL TAPE BUT CHIP RALLY CONTINUES. Samsung Q1 prelim 57.2 trillion won ($37.9 billion) operating profit, +755% YoY. SMH $506.26 Tue flat 1D, +32.9% 1M — global AI capex thesis durable. The chip rally is INDEPENDENT of crisis: chip names would be up +20-30% 1M with crisis OR without crisis on Samsung/SK Hynix earnings cycle. CRITICAL: yesterday's Brent $100.47 print was yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 / Tue $109.39 — oil REVERSED UP, not down. The 'Branch 3 marginal positive +0.3-0.5% via FII flow reversal' overlay from yesterday is empirically falsified for the Branch 3 mechanism — but the FII flow reversal itself (rupee ₹94.17 Tue Day 5 strengthening; FPIs withdrew ₹48,213 cr first 10 days April per NSDL but Mon close was India-domestic-flow-led) shows mixed signal. Q4 FY26 earnings season weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro) is the dominant driver; the global AI capex bid (TSMC capex +32% to $54B; Samsung Q1 record; helium falsification) provides spillover support but does NOT resolve domestic Q4 margin pressure. Indian IT remains margin-pressure trade.

signals.content: Indian IT index underperforming Nifty. Q4 FY26 earnings weak — Infosys guidance cut, HDFC Bank NIM compression (not IT but signals broader weakness). The crisis-to-IT chain: oil spike → US inflation stays elevated → Fed holds rates → US GDP revised down to 1.8% → enterprise IT spending cuts → Indian IT revenue pressure. SMH (semiconductors) -9.2% monthly. Nifty IT index tracking ~15% below pre-crisis levels. (Sources: Business Standard, yfinance, company filings)

Indian IT sector faces worsening indirect crisis exposure through US recession risk. The transmission chain: oil inflation -> Fed can't cut (52% pricing Fed HIKE by year-end) -> US growth pressure (ISM stagflation signal: Prices 78.3, employment contracting) -> IT client spending cuts. SMH (semiconductors) -1.7% monthly. No specific new IT sector developments but the macro backdrop has deteriorated significantly since March 28. US bond market pricing growth deterioration (10Y yields fell on peace rally day — bonds pricing recession while equities celebrated). Q4 FY26 earnings season approaching — guidance risk elevated. (Sources: yfinance, ISM, CNBC)

Indian IT services sector operating but exposed to (a) US demand slowdown if war drags into Q2 earnings, (b) rupee volatility (Rs 92.93 holding 93). No major pre-warnings yet. Q1 earnings season starts April 10 globally — Indian IT will react to US bank earnings (JPM, GS, MS, Wells, BAC report April 10-15). Watch TCS/Infosys language on US discretionary spending in upcoming results.

Day 45, Ceasefire Day 5. SMH $436.88 (+1.5% Friday, +8.9% 1M). Indian IT sector faces indirect crisis exposure: oil spike → US inflation → Fed can't cut → US growth pressure → IT demand weakness. Q4 FY26 earnings season underway — Infosys and TCS guidance revisions expected. The Islamabad talks failure compounds US recession risk through the oil→inflation→Fed pathway. Helium semiconductor cliff bear case is dead (TrendForce window expired, Samsung LTAs secured). (Source: yfinance, markets.md)

TCS Q4 FY26 ACTUAL RESULTS — AI MATERIALITY THRESHOLD CROSSED [CONFIRMED, announced April 9 post-market]. Revenue $7,621M (+1.5% QoQ, +1.2% constant currency); FY26 operating margin 25.0% (+70bps YoY); net margin 19.8% (+80bps YoY). TCV: Q4 $12.0B, FY26 $40.7B with 3 mega deals. Annualized AI revenues surpassed $2.3B (first reveal of AI standalone revenue disclosure). This is the single most important Indian IT data point of Q4 FY26: the AI business has moved from 'pilot + proof-of-concept' to 'material revenue contribution' — $2.3B annualized is ~7% of TCS total revenue. Infosys reports April 23 — consensus expects revenue Rs 46,739 crore (+14.2% YoY) and PAT Rs 7,791 crore (+10.8%). Street expects FY27 guide 1.5-4.5% CC YoY bear case / 3-6% bull case. Read-through for Wipro, HCL, TechM: AI materiality test is now live. TSMC $18.2B Q1 net income + raised CapEx guide validates that the underlying AI infrastructure build continues to accelerate — Indian IT is the services-integration layer, not the hardware layer, so the chain is: semiconductor capex → hyperscaler infra → enterprise AI adoption → Indian IT implementation services. Revenue sequencing: 2026 capex → 2027 services growth. (Sources: TCS Q4 FY26 release April 9, Business Today, Univest, BusinessToday)

INDIAN IT SECTOR WEAK WED OPEN [REPORTED, Business Today intraday]. Infosys, HCL, Wipro, Tech Mahindra drifting on US recession-risk concerns (indirect crisis transmission: oil-inflation → Fed cannot cut → US growth pressure → Indian IT client budget cuts). Not directly crisis-linked. Contributes ~30% of Nifty -0.7%.

HCL TECH TUMBLED ~9% ON MARCH-QUARTER EARNINGS MISS — DOMESTIC DRAG ON NIFTY/SENSEX SEALED [CONFIRMED via etvbharat]. HCL Technologies' Q4 FY26 missed expectations, dragging the IT sector cohort: Infosys, HCL, Wipro, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, HCL Tech, Apollo Hospitals all selling Wednesday. Q4 FY26 earnings season delivering MIXED results — TCS Q4 was strong on AI revenues materiality; HCL miss is the first major guidance disappointment. Domestic component (~50% of Nifty -0.8% / Sensex -1.0% Wed) does NOT reverse on Iran resolution. Bank Nifty -0.4% (less hit than IT-heavy indices) — ICICI Q4 NIM-protection holding. The capital-flight decoupling Day 3 is ~50% domestic earnings (HCL Tech-style misses) ~50% crisis-driven FII outflows; Nimit's deployment thesis must separate these. Even a clean ceasefire renewal Sunday April 26 only recovers ~50% of cumulative Indian drawdown — domestic earnings drag persists.

Branch 3 confirmation marginal positive for Indian IT via FII flow reversal [REPORTED]. Brent gap-down to $100.47 + RBI managed-depreciation breathing room + Elara Capital first +$106M weekly inflow in 7 weeks = FII deceleration crystallising into reversal. Indian IT Q4 FY26 earnings weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro) is the dominant driver; Branch 3 is +0.3-0.5% relief overlay. Global AI capex thesis intact via TSMC capex +32% to $54B for 2026 + Asian chip rally Monday morning. (Sources: Elara Capital, Business Standard, TrendForce, yfinance)
signals.verification: REPORTED — Branch 3 marginal effect overlay on dominant Q4 weakness driver.

signals.content: SAMSUNG Q1 PRELIM RECORD REINFORCES AI-CAPEX BACKDROP; BRANCH 3 OIL THESIS FALSIFIED [CONFIRMED]. Samsung Q1 prelim 57.2 trillion won ($37.9 billion) operating profit, +755% YoY — first Korean company above 50T won quarterly OP. AI memory + HBM-driven. SMH $506.26 Tue flat 1D, +32.9% 1M; KOSPI +26.3% 1M; Taiwan TAIEX +19.4% 1M. CORRECTION: yesterday's Brent $100.47 print was yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 / Tue $109.39 — oil REVERSED UP, not down. The 'Branch 3 marginal positive +0.3-0.5% relief via FII flow reversal' overlay from yesterday is mechanism-falsified (no Branch 3 unwind), but Samsung Q1 record + global AI capex (TSMC capex +32% to $54B) is the bigger structural input — Indian IT will benefit from spillover even without Branch 3. FII flow shows mixed signal: rupee ₹94.17 Tue Day 5 strengthening but FPIs still withdrew ₹48,213 cr first 10 days April per NSDL; Nifty Mon close +0.81% was India-domestic-flow-led, not FII-led. Q4 FY26 earnings weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro) remains dominant driver. (Sources: Samsung preliminary Q1 release, NSDL, Business Standard, TrendForce, TradingEconomics)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Samsung Q1 prelim + CONFIRMED Brent correction + CONFIRMED FII flow data.

edges.context: 60-70% of Indian IT revenue comes from US clients. S&P weakness + ISM stagflation signal -> enterprise spending cuts -> Indian IT revenue decline.
IT is the largest sector in Nifty by weight (~15%). IT weakness drags the index even if other sectors hold.
Fed rate path determines US growth outlook which determines IT spending. Fed holding/hiking rates = growth pressure = IT headwind. Market now pricing 52% Fed hike probability.

trigger_points.condition: Major Indian IT company issues profit warning citing US demand slowdown
trigger_points.mechanism: Would confirm the crisis cascade reaching Indian IT earnings. Nifty would take a direct hit. Rupee weakens further as IT is India's largest forex earner.
